rhetorical_signals: Speaker hedged: "I don't know. One of the reasons I'm interested in collaborating..."
Speaker admitted lack of data: "We haven't gone down that road yet"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: KLF5 role in metastasis not yet validated in patients

key_data_gaps: No in‑human clinical data for KLF5 targeting
No functional validation of epigenetic entropy as therapeutic biomarker

crispro_opportunity.description: KLF5‑driven epigenetic remodeling appears to enable pancreatic cancer metastasis; targeting this pathway could block metastatic outgrowth.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described that knockdown of KLF5 restores heterochromatin and inhibits growth of metastasis‑derived xenografts.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the transcription‑factor‑mediated chromatin network to predict combinatorial inhibitors that suppress KLF5‑driven programs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of a clinical assay to identify tumors with high KLF5 expression or epigenetic entropy for patient selection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted KLF5 is highly expressed in distant metastases and correlates with poor survival in TCGA data.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to derive a gene‑expression entropy signature linked to KLF5 activity for companion diagnostic development.

unresolved_questions: What microenvironmental stressors trigger KLF5 up‑regulation in primary tumors?
Can circulating nucleosome signatures reflect the epigenetic entropy state of metastatic precursors?
